question_en: What is the longest river in the world?
context_en: The Nile, at ~6,650 km, is considered the longest river. It crosses 11 African countries before flowing into the Mediterranean.

question_en: In which country is the Eiffel Tower?
context_en: The Eiffel Tower is in Paris, France. It was built by Gustave Eiffel for the 1889 World's Fair and stands 330 m tall.

question_en: What is the largest country in the world by area?
context_en: Russia is the largest country, at 17.1 million km². It spans 11 time zones and represents 11% of the world's land area.

question_en: What is the capital of Australia?
context_en: Canberra has been Australia's capital since 1913, purpose-designed to resolve the rivalry between Sydney and Melbourne.

question_en: Which is the largest ocean in the world?
context_en: The Pacific Ocean is the largest and deepest, covering more than 30% of the Earth's surface. Its deepest point is the Mariana Trench.

question_en: What is the highest mountain in the world?
context_en: Mount Everest (8,849 m) is the highest mountain in the world, in the Himalayas, on the border between Nepal and China.

question_en: What is the largest desert in the world?
context_en: The largest desert is the Arctic (13.9 million km²). The Sahara is the largest hot desert in the world at 9.2 million km².

question_en: How many continents are there in the world?
context_en: According to the most widespread model, there are 7 continents: Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Europe, Oceania, North America and South America.

question_en: What is the largest lake in the world?
context_en: The Caspian Sea (371,000 km²) is technically the largest lake. Lake Superior is the largest freshwater lake. Baikal is the deepest.

question_en: What is the smallest country in the world?
context_en: Vatican City (0.44 km²) is the smallest country in the world, enclosed within Rome. It is the seat of the Catholic Church.

question_en: What is the capital of Japan?
context_en: Tokyo is the capital of Japan and the most populous city in the world, with more than 37 million people in its metropolitan area.

question_en: What is the longest river in Europe?
context_en: The Volga (3,531 km) is the longest river in Europe, rising in the Valdai Hills and flowing into the Caspian Sea. It is a symbol of Russia.

question_en: In which country is the Amazon?
context_en: Most of the Amazon (60%) is in Brazil. It has the largest river basin in the world and is home to 10% of the planet's species.

question_en: What is the most populous country in the world?
context_en: India overtook China as the most populous country in 2023, with more than 1.4 billion inhabitants.

question_en: Which country has the most land borders?
context_en: China shares borders with 14 countries, tied with Russia (also 14). China has the longest land border in the world.

question_en: What is the highest peak in the Americas?
context_en: Aconcagua (6,960 m) in Argentina is the highest peak in the Americas and the Western Hemisphere.

question_en: What is the capital of Canada?
context_en: Ottawa has been the capital of Canada since 1857. Although Toronto and Montreal are larger, Ottawa was chosen for its position between English and French speakers.

question_en: What is the longest country in the world from north to south?
context_en: Chile is ~4,300 km from north to south but only 177 km wide. It stretches from the Atacama Desert to Patagonia.

question_en: Where is the deepest point in the oceans?
context_en: The Mariana Trench (Pacific) is the deepest point, at 11,034 m in the 'Challenger Deep'. It was first explored in 1960.

question_en: What is the smallest continent?
context_en: Australia is the smallest continent (7.7 million km²). If Oceania is counted as a continent, it includes thousands of Pacific islands.

question_en: Which country has the most islands in the world?
context_en: Sweden has more than 220,000 islands. Indonesia has the most extensive and populated islands (17,000 islands), with 6,000 inhabited.

question_en: What is the longest river in South America?
context_en: The Amazon (6,400 km) is the longest river in the Americas and the largest by discharge in the world, accounting for 20% of the fresh water reaching the oceans.

question_en: In which country is the city of Dubai?
context_en: Dubai is in the United Arab Emirates. It is the most populous city in the country and home to the tallest building in the world: the Burj Khalifa (828 m).

question_en: What is the capital of Brazil?
context_en: Brasília has been the capital of Brazil since 1960, built from scratch to decentralize the country. Rio de Janeiro was the capital until 1960.

question_en: What is the highest volcano in the world?
context_en: Ojos del Salado (6,893 m), on the border between Chile and Argentina, is the highest volcano in the world.

question_en: In which country is the city of Istanbul?
context_en: Istanbul is in Turkey, straddling Europe and Asia. It was the capital of the Ottoman Empire (Constantinople) and earlier of the Byzantine Empire.

question_en: What is the largest country in Africa?
context_en: Algeria is the largest country in Africa (2.38 million km²), after the division of Sudan in 2011. Its capital is Algiers.

question_en: How many countries does South America have?
context_en: South America has 12 countries: Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Chile, Colombia, Ecuador, Guyana, Paraguay, Peru, Suriname, Uruguay and Venezuela.

question_en: What is the highest point in Europe?
context_en: Elbrus (5,642 m) in the Russian Caucasus is the highest point in Europe according to many geographers. Mont Blanc (4,808 m) is the highest in the Alps.

question_en: What is the capital of Argentina?
context_en: Buenos Aires is the capital of Argentina and the largest city in the country, with about 15 million people in its metropolitan area.

question_en: Which country has the greatest biodiversity in the world?
context_en: Brazil is home to 20% of the planet's species. The Amazon is the most biodiverse ecosystem in the world. Colombia is the second most biodiverse country.

question_en: What is the longest river in Asia?
context_en: The Yangtze (6,300 km) is the longest river in Asia and the third in the world. The Three Gorges Dam, the largest in the world, is on this river.

question_en: How many countries are there in Africa?
context_en: Africa has 54 recognized countries, making it the continent with the most countries in the world. Nigeria is the most populous and Algeria the largest.

question_en: Which strait separates Europe from Africa?
context_en: The Strait of Gibraltar separates Spain from Morocco with just 14 km at its narrowest point. It connects the Atlantic with the Mediterranean.

question_en: Which country has the most official languages?
context_en: South Africa has 11 official languages: Zulu, Xhosa, Afrikaans, English, Sotho, Tswana, Southern Sotho, Tsonga, Swazi, Venda and Ndebele.

question_en: Where is the Atacama Desert?
context_en: The Atacama Desert is in Chile, on the Pacific coast. It is the driest non-polar desert in the world: some areas have not recorded rain in centuries.

question_en: What is the highest waterfall in the world?
context_en: Angel Falls (Venezuela) is the highest waterfall in the world, with a free fall of 979 m. It was discovered by aviator James Angel in 1933.

question_en: Which is the most developed country in the world by HDI?
context_en: Norway usually leads the Human Development Index (HDI). It has the best indicators of health, education and living standards in the world.

question_en: Which ocean washes the coasts of Spain?
options_en: The Atlantic, the Mediterranean and the Cantabrian
context_en: Spain has coasts on the Atlantic (west and northwest), the Cantabrian Sea (north) and the Mediterranean (east and south). The Cantabrian is technically part of the Atlantic.

question_en: What is the highest city in the world?
context_en: La Paz (Bolivia) at 3,640 m is the highest de facto capital. The Bolivian city of El Alto, at 4,150 m, is the highest large city. La Paz is the seat of government.

question_en: Which country has the most territory in the Arctic?
context_en: Russia has the largest presence in the Arctic, with almost half of its coast on the Arctic Ocean. It has the largest icebreaker fleet in the world.

question_en: What is the capital of Morocco?
context_en: Rabat has been the capital of Morocco since 1912, during the French protectorate. Casablanca is the largest city and economic center.

question_en: Which continent has no countries?
context_en: Antarctica has no countries or permanent population. It is governed by the Antarctic Treaty (1959), which reserves it for peace and science.

question_en: What is the capital of Turkey?
context_en: Ankara has been the capital of Turkey since 1923, when Atatürk chose it for its central position in Anatolia. Istanbul is the largest city.

question_en: Which country has the longest coastline in the world?
context_en: Canada has the longest coastline in the world (202,080 km), thanks to its thousands of islands and indentations. Russia is second.

question_en: What is the smallest sea in the world?
context_en: The Sea of Azov (39,000 km²) is the smallest sea in the world. It lies in southeastern Ukraine and southwestern Russia, connected to the Black Sea.

question_en: What is the capital of Egypt?
context_en: Cairo is the capital of Egypt and the largest city in Africa and the Arab world, with about 20 million inhabitants.

question_en: Which country has more than half of its territory below sea level?
context_en: The Netherlands has 26% of its territory below sea level. It has built an advanced system of dikes and polders to reclaim land from the sea.

question_en: What is the deepest lake in the world?
context_en: Lake Baikal (Russia) is 1,642 m deep, the deepest in the world. It contains 20% of the world's unfrozen fresh surface water.

question_en: Which is the most populous city in Europe?
context_en: Moscow is the most populous city in Europe, with more than 12 million inhabitants in the city (20 million in the metropolitan area). It is the capital of Russia.

question_en: What is the largest island in the world?
context_en: Greenland (2.16 million km²) is the largest island in the world. It belongs to Denmark but has wide autonomy. 80% is covered in ice.

question_en: Which is the richest country in the world by GDP per capita?
context_en: Luxembourg usually leads the world in GDP per capita, at more than $120,000. Its wealth is based on financial services and steel.

question_en: What is the highest mountain in Africa?
context_en: Kilimanjaro (5,895 m) in Tanzania is the highest mountain in Africa. It is an inactive volcano with three cones: Kibo, Mawenzi and Shira.

question_en: Which sea separates Europe from North Africa?
context_en: The Mediterranean Sea separates Europe from North Africa. It covers 2.5 million km² and is almost an inland sea, with little connection to the oceans.

question_en: What is the capital of South Korea?
context_en: Seoul is the capital of South Korea, with more than 10 million inhabitants. It is one of the most technologically advanced cities in the world.

question_en: In which country is the Ganges River?
context_en: The Ganges rises in the Himalayas and flows mainly through India before reaching the Bay of Bengal. It is the sacred river of Hinduism.

question_en: What is the lowest point on Earth (on land)?
context_en: The Dead Sea (Israel/Jordan/Palestine) is 430 m below sea level, the lowest point on dry land. Its high salinity prevents you from sinking.

question_en: What is the capital of Nigeria?
context_en: Abuja has been the capital of Nigeria since 1991, built from scratch to replace Lagos as capital. Lagos remains the largest and most economic city.

question_en: Which country has the largest oil reserves in the world?
context_en: Venezuela has the largest proven oil reserves (303 billion barrels), followed by Saudi Arabia. However, it is difficult to extract.

question_en: What is the capital of Portugal?
context_en: Lisbon, located at the mouth of the Tagus River, has been the capital and largest city of Portugal since 1255.

question_en: What is the capital of Germany?
context_en: Berlin is the capital and largest city of Germany. It became the capital again after reunification in 1990 (previously it was Bonn in West Germany).

question_en: What is the capital of Italy?
context_en: Rome, the 'Eternal City', has been the capital of Italy since 1871. It contains the Vatican, seat of the Catholic Church.

question_en: What is the capital of Russia?
context_en: Moscow has been the capital of Russia since 1918 (when the Bolsheviks moved it from Saint Petersburg). It is the most populous city in Europe.

question_en: On which continent is Madagascar?
context_en: Madagascar is an island nation in the Indian Ocean, off the southeast coast of Africa. It is the fourth largest island in the world.

question_en: Which is the largest country in the European Union?
context_en: France is the largest country in the EU with about 643,000 km² (including overseas territories), after the departure of the United Kingdom.

question_en: Which mountain range separates Europe from Asia?
context_en: The Ural Mountains are considered the natural border between Europe and Asia, crossing Russia from north to south.

question_en: What is the capital of Sweden?
context_en: Stockholm is the capital and largest city of Sweden, built on 14 islands. Host of the Nobel Prizes (except the Peace Prize).

question_en: What is the capital of Norway?
context_en: Oslo is the capital and largest city of Norway, located at the end of the Oslofjord. Host of the Nobel Peace Prize.

question_en: What is the capital of Finland?
context_en: Helsinki has been the capital and largest city of Finland since 1812. It is located on the south coast, facing the Gulf of Finland.

question_en: What is the capital of Denmark?
context_en: Copenhagen is the capital of Denmark, on the island of Zealand. Famous for the Little Mermaid and the Tivoli Gardens.

question_en: What is the capital of Belgium?
context_en: Brussels is the capital of Belgium and the de facto seat of the European Union and NATO.

question_en: What is the capital of the Netherlands?
context_en: Amsterdam is the constitutional capital of the Netherlands, although the government and royalty reside in The Hague.

question_en: What is the capital of Switzerland?
context_en: Bern is the de facto capital of Switzerland (federal city). Switzerland has no official capital declared in its Constitution.

question_en: In which country is the city of Prague?
context_en: Prague, the 'city of a hundred spires', is the capital and largest city of the Czech Republic. The Vltava River runs through it.

question_en: What is the capital of Hungary?
context_en: Budapest was formed in 1873 by the union of Buda, Óbuda and Pest. It is divided by the Danube River.

question_en: In which country is the city of Vienna?
context_en: Vienna is the capital of Austria, historically the seat of the House of Habsburg and one of the great European musical capitals.

question_en: What is the capital of Poland?
context_en: Warsaw is the capital and largest city of Poland, crossed by the Vistula River. It was almost completely destroyed in World War II.

question_en: What is the capital of Greece?
context_en: Athens, cradle of democracy and Western philosophy, is the capital of Greece. Famous for the Acropolis.

question_en: In which country is the city of Marrakech?
context_en: Marrakech is one of the four imperial cities of Morocco. Known for the Jemaa el-Fnaa square.

question_en: What is the capital of Ethiopia?
context_en: Addis Ababa (which in Amharic means 'new flower') is the capital of Ethiopia and the seat of the African Union.

question_en: What is the capital of Kenya?
context_en: Nairobi is the capital and largest city of Kenya. It is the seat of several UN agencies in Africa.

question_en: What is the capital of South Africa (seat of the executive)?
context_en: South Africa has three capitals: Pretoria (executive), Cape Town (legislative) and Bloemfontein (judicial).

question_en: In which country is Petra?
context_en: Petra, the ancient Nabataean capital carved into the rock, is in Jordan. It has been a World Heritage Site since 1985.

question_en: What is the capital of Israel (internationally recognized)?
context_en: Israel proclaims Jerusalem as its capital, but most states and the UN place its de facto capital in Tel Aviv.

question_en: What is the capital of Saudi Arabia?
context_en: Riyadh is the capital and largest city of Saudi Arabia, located in the center of the Arabian Peninsula.

question_en: What is the capital of Iran?
context_en: Tehran has been the capital of Iran since 1796. It has about 9 million inhabitants in the city and 16 in the metropolitan area.

question_en: What is the capital of Iraq?
context_en: Baghdad, founded in 762, was the capital of the Abbasid Caliphate. It has been the capital and largest city of Iraq since its independence.

question_en: What is the capital of the Philippines?
context_en: Manila is the capital of the Philippines and the seat of government. The most populous city in the country is Quezon City.

question_en: What is the capital of Thailand?
context_en: Bangkok (Krung Thep in Thai) is the capital of Thailand. Its full name is one of the longest in the world.

question_en: What is the capital of Vietnam?
context_en: Hanoi has been the capital of Vietnam since the 1976 reunification. Ho Chi Minh City (Saigon) is the most populous city.

question_en: What is the capital of Indonesia?
context_en: Jakarta is the capital of Indonesia, on the island of Java. The government has planned to move the capital to 'Nusantara' in Borneo.

question_en: What is the capital of Malaysia?
context_en: Kuala Lumpur is the capital and largest city of Malaysia. The Petronas Towers, its emblem, were the tallest in the world (1998-2004).

question_en: In which country are the Galápagos Islands?
context_en: The Galápagos are an archipelago of Ecuador in the Pacific. Charles Darwin studied them in 1835, developing his theory of evolution.

question_en: What is the capital of Peru?
context_en: Lima was founded by Francisco Pizarro in 1535 as the 'City of Kings'. It is the capital and largest city of Peru.

question_en: What is the capital of Chile?
context_en: Santiago has been the capital of Chile since 1541, although the National Congress has its seat in Valparaíso.

question_en: What is the capital of Colombia?
context_en: Bogotá is the capital and largest city of Colombia. Its official name is Bogotá D.C. (Capital District), located at 2,640 m of altitude.

question_en: What is the capital of Venezuela?
context_en: Caracas is the capital and largest city of Venezuela. It was founded in 1567 by Diego de Losada.

question_en: What is the capital of Uruguay?
context_en: Montevideo is the capital of Uruguay. It is home to about a third of the country's population.

question_en: What is the capital of Paraguay?
context_en: Asunción, founded in 1537, is the capital and largest city of Paraguay. It is one of the oldest cities in South America.

question_en: What is the capital of Bolivia (seat of government)?
context_en: La Paz is the seat of government and the most populous city of Bolivia. Sucre is the constitutional capital.

question_en: What is the capital of Ecuador?
context_en: Quito, located at 2,850 m of altitude, is the capital of Ecuador. It was the first city declared a World Heritage Site by UNESCO (1978).

question_en: What is the capital of Cuba?
context_en: Havana is the capital and largest city of Cuba, founded in 1519. Its historic center is a World Heritage Site.

question_en: What is the capital of Mexico?
context_en: Mexico City (CDMX), founded on the ancient Tenochtitlan, is one of the most populous metropolises in the world.

question_en: Which country has Reykjavik as its capital?
context_en: Reykjavik is the capital and largest city of Iceland, as well as the northernmost capital of a state in the world.

question_en: In which country is the region of Patagonia?
context_en: Patagonia is divided between Argentina (most of it) and Chile, at the southern tip of South America.

question_en: Which mountain range crosses South America from north to south?
context_en: The Andes mountain range runs through 7 South American countries for 7,000 km. It is the longest continental mountain range in the world.

question_en: What is the highest navigable lake in the world?
context_en: Lake Titicaca (3,812 m) between Peru and Bolivia is the highest commercially navigable lake in the world.

question_en: Which strait separates South America from Antarctica?
context_en: The Drake Passage, between Cape Horn and Antarctica, is famous for its violent storms.

question_en: In which desert are the highest dunes in the world?
context_en: In the Namib Desert (Namibia) are some of the highest dunes in the world, such as those of Sossusvlei.

question_en: What is the longest river in Africa?
context_en: The Nile, at about 6,650 km, is the longest river in Africa and is often cited as the longest in the world (competing with the Amazon).

question_en: In which country are Victoria Falls?
context_en: Victoria Falls, on the Zambezi River, lie on the border between Zimbabwe and Zambia. Called Mosi-oa-Tunya by the locals.

question_en: In which country are the Iguazú Falls?
context_en: The Iguazú Falls are on the border between Argentina (province of Misiones) and Brazil (Paraná).

question_en: What is the longest mountain range in the world (underwater)?
context_en: The Mid-Atlantic Ridge runs through the Atlantic Ocean from north to south, ~16,000 km, forming the boundary between tectonic plates.

question_en: Which is the most populous country in Africa?
context_en: Nigeria, with more than 220 million inhabitants, is the most populous country in Africa and the sixth in the world.

question_en: What is the official currency of the United Kingdom?
context_en: The pound sterling (GBP) is the official currency of the United Kingdom, one of the oldest currencies in continuous use.

question_en: Which country is shaped like a boot?
context_en: Italy has a characteristic boot shape that extends into the Mediterranean.

question_en: Which peninsula do Spain and Portugal share?
context_en: Spain and Portugal share the Iberian Peninsula, along with Andorra, Gibraltar and a small part of France.

question_en: Which sea separates Spain from Africa?
context_en: The Alboran Sea (western part of the Mediterranean) separates Spain from Morocco. The Strait of Gibraltar connects the Mediterranean with the Atlantic.

question_en: What is the official currency of Japan?
context_en: The yen (¥) has been the official currency of Japan since 1871. It is the third most traded currency in the world.

question_en: Which currency does Switzerland use?
context_en: Switzerland uses the Swiss franc (CHF). It is the only official currency of the country and of Liechtenstein.

question_en: What is the driest desert in the world?
context_en: The Atacama Desert, in northern Chile, is the driest non-polar desert in the world, with areas that have recorded no rain in centuries.

question_en: Which ocean current cools the coasts of Chile and Peru?
context_en: The Humboldt Current is a cold current that runs along the west coast of South America. It influences the region's climate and fishing.

question_en: What is the capital of Singapore?
context_en: Singapore is a city-state: the country and the capital coincide.

question_en: What is the capital of Mongolia?
context_en: Ulaanbaatar is the capital and largest city of Mongolia, home to nearly half the country's population.

question_en: What is the capital of Kazakhstan?
context_en: Astana (briefly renamed Nur-Sultan between 2019 and 2022) has been the capital of Kazakhstan since 1997.

question_en: Which ocean washes the east coast of the U.S.?
context_en: The east coast of the United States faces the Atlantic Ocean, while the west coast faces the Pacific.

question_en: Which strait separates Asia from America?
context_en: The Bering Strait, about 82 km wide, separates Russia (Siberia) from the U.S. (Alaska) and connects the Arctic with the Pacific.

question_en: Which strait controls the entrance to the Persian Gulf?
context_en: The Strait of Hormuz, between Iran and Oman, is a key sea route for the world's oil transport.

question_en: How many islands make up the Japanese archipelago (main ones)?
context_en: The four main islands of Japan are Honshu, Hokkaido, Kyushu and Shikoku. The country has a total of about 14,000 islands.

question_en: Which is the largest Mediterranean island?
context_en: Sicily, at about 25,700 km², is the largest island in the Mediterranean. It belongs to Italy.

question_en: Which country has Helsinki as its capital?
context_en: Helsinki is the capital and largest city of Finland, located on the south coast facing the Gulf of Finland.

question_en: Which mountain range crosses North Africa?
context_en: The Atlas Mountains stretch across Morocco, Algeria and Tunisia, separating the Mediterranean coast from the Sahara Desert.

question_en: Which is the smallest country in South America (by area)?
context_en: Suriname, at about 163,820 km², is the smallest country in South America. Formerly Dutch Guiana.

question_en: Which European country is landlocked?
context_en: Switzerland is landlocked, as are Austria, Hungary, Czechia, Slovakia, Luxembourg, Liechtenstein, San Marino, the Vatican, Belarus, etc.

question_en: Which countries are landlocked in South America?
context_en: Bolivia and Paraguay are the only South American countries without sea access. Bolivia lost it in the War of the Pacific (1879-1884).

question_en: What is the capital of New Zealand?
context_en: Wellington is the capital of New Zealand, although Auckland is the most populous city.

question_en: Which mountain range separates France from Spain?
context_en: The Pyrenees form the natural border between France and Spain, about 430 km long.

question_en: Which mountain range separates Italy from central Europe?
context_en: The Alps form a natural barrier between Italy and the rest of central Europe. Mont Blanc (4,808 m) is its highest peak.

question_en: Which is the most populous island of Indonesia?
context_en: Java is the most populous island in the world (~150 million). It is home to Indonesia's capital, Jakarta.

question_en: Which currency does Sweden use?
context_en: Sweden, although an EU member, has not adopted the euro. Its currency is the Swedish krona (SEK).

question_en: What is the official currency of India?
context_en: The Indian rupee (INR), symbol ₹, is the official currency of India.

question_en: Which country has Beijing as its capital?
context_en: Beijing has been the capital of the People's Republic of China since 1949 and one of the most populous cities in the world.

question_en: Which Chinese city is the largest by urban population?
context_en: Shanghai, with more than 25 million inhabitants in the urban area, is the most populous city in China.

question_en: What is the longest river in West Africa?
context_en: The Niger, at about 4,180 km, is the third longest river in Africa and the main one in West Africa.

question_en: In which country is the city of Samarkand?
context_en: Samarkand, a key city on the Silk Road, is in Uzbekistan. It was the capital of Tamerlane's empire.

question_en: In which ocean is Easter Island?
context_en: Easter Island (Rapa Nui) is in the South Pacific. It belongs to Chile and is famous for its moai statues.

question_en: Which country owns Greenland?
context_en: Greenland is an autonomous territory of the Kingdom of Denmark. It is the largest island in the world.

question_en: Which is the smallest ocean?
context_en: The Arctic Ocean, at about 14 million km², is the smallest and shallowest of the five oceans.

question_en: What is the largest lake in Africa?
context_en: Lake Victoria, at about 68,870 km², is the largest lake in Africa and the second largest freshwater lake in the world.

question_en: In which country is the Pampas region?
context_en: The Pampas is the great Argentine plain (it also extends to Uruguay and southern Brazil). Center of agricultural and livestock production.

question_en: What is the capital of Bangladesh?
context_en: Dhaka is the capital and largest city of Bangladesh, with a metropolitan area of more than 22 million inhabitants.

question_en: Which country has the largest area of tropical forest?
context_en: Brazil is home to approximately 60% of the Amazon rainforest, the largest tropical forest in the world.

question_en: Which is the second most populous country in the world?
context_en: Since 2023, India has overtaken China in population. Before that date, China was the most populous and India the second.
